county_metadata.record_losses: 1849 courthouse fire destroyed most early records; additional losses in 1865 Civil War period
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Florida county formed 1822; formed during territorial period (pre-statehood); Panhandle region
county_metadata.research_tips: Florida State Archives and Jackson County Clerk (Marianna). One of Florida's original territorial counties (1822), formed from Escambia. BURNED COUNTY — 1849 courthouse fire destroyed most early records and additional losses during the Civil War. Jackson was a major plantation county — largest enslaved population in West Florida. Battle of Marianna (1864) records. Florida Caverns State Park area. Jackson County Floridan newspaper archives. Check Escambia for pre-1822 records. Florida State Archives has some surviving state copies.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Florida county formed 1827; formed during territorial period (pre-statehood); North Florida
county_metadata.research_tips: Florida State Archives and Jefferson County Clerk (Monticello). Formed 1827 from Leon — one of Florida's older counties. Major plantation county in the cotton belt. Jefferson had the highest percentage of enslaved persons of any Florida county. Monticello Opera House area. Jefferson County Historical Association has local collections. Aucilla River archaeological sites. Pre-1827 records in Leon. Check Florida State Archives for personal property tax lists that enumerate enslaved persons by slaveholder.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Florida county formed 1856; antebellum county; North Florida
county_metadata.research_tips: Florida State Archives and Lafayette County Clerk (Mayo). Formed 1856 from Madison. Florida's smallest county by population. Pre-1856 records in Madison. Suwannee River area. Very limited local historical society resources. Check Madison County records and Florida State Archives for earlier family records. Lafayette County was named for the Marquis de Lafayette. Timber, farming, and turpentine industry heritage.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Florida county formed 1887; post-Civil War formation; Central Florida
county_metadata.research_tips: Florida State Archives and Lake County Clerk (Tavares). Formed 1887 from Sumter and Orange. Pre-1887 records in parent counties. Lake County Historical Society and Museum (Tavares) has local genealogical collections. Mount Dora area. Clermont area citrus heritage. Numerous named lakes in the county. Railroad history important — Plant System and Atlantic Coast Line records. Groveland and Mascotte area agricultural records.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Florida county formed 1887; post-Civil War formation; South Florida
county_metadata.research_tips: Florida State Archives and Lee County Clerk (Fort Myers). Formed 1887 from Monroe — parent county for Collier (1923), Hendry (1923), and Charlotte (1921). Fort Myers was Thomas Edison and Henry Ford's winter home. Lee County was very sparsely populated before the 1880s. Southwest Florida Historical Society has local materials. Calusa heritage — Mound Key archaeological site. Fort Myers-Lee County Public Library has genealogical resources. Cattle ranching and fishing industry records.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Florida county formed 1824; formed during territorial period (pre-statehood); Panhandle region
county_metadata.research_tips: Florida State Archives and Leon County Clerk (Tallahassee). Formed 1824 from Gadsden. Tallahassee is the state capital; the Florida State Archives (primary state repository) is located here. Major plantation county with significant enslaved population. Florida State University and FAMU (Florida A&M) archives have local and African American history collections. Pre-1824 records in Gadsden (and earlier in Jackson/Escambia). Tallahassee-Leon County Historical Society. Mission San Luis archaeological records. Spanish mission period (1633+) records at P.K. Yonge Library.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Florida county formed 1845; formed during territorial period (pre-statehood); North Florida
county_metadata.research_tips: Florida State Archives and Levy County Clerk (Bronson). Formed 1845 — the year of Florida's statehood — from Alachua and Marion. Cedar Key was a major port and railroad terminus. David Levy Yulee (Florida's first US Senator) — Cedar Key railroad records. Levy County Historical Society has limited materials. Cedar Keys Museum. Withlacoochee River area settlement. Check Alachua and Marion for pre-1845 records. Gulf Hammock area homesteading records.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Florida county formed 1855; antebellum county; Panhandle region
county_metadata.research_tips: Florida State Archives and Liberty County Clerk (Bristol). Formed 1855 from Gadsden and Franklin. Small, rural, sparsely populated Panhandle county. Pre-1855 records in Gadsden and Franklin. Apalachicola National Forest covers much of the county. Torreya State Park area. Liberty County has very limited local historical society resources. Check Florida State Archives for homestead and land entry records. Calhoun and Liberty historically share many families.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Florida county formed 1827; formed during territorial period (pre-statehood); North Florida
county_metadata.research_tips: Florida State Archives and Madison County Clerk (Madison). Formed 1827 from Jefferson — one of Florida's older counties. Major plantation and tobacco county in the cotton belt. Pre-1827 records in Jefferson. Madison County Historical Society has local materials. Wardlaw-Smith-Goza Conference Center (antebellum mansion). Significant enslaved population — check Florida State Archives for personal property tax lists. Suwannee River area. Parent county for Taylor (1856), Lafayette (1856), and Dixie (1921).

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Florida county formed 1855; antebellum county; Central Florida
county_metadata.research_tips: Florida State Archives and Manatee County Clerk (Bradenton). Formed 1855 from Hillsborough. Major parent county — DeSoto (1887) and Sarasota (1921) carved from Manatee. Manatee River area pioneer families. Gamble Plantation Historic State Park (antebellum sugar plantation). Manatee County Historical Records Library (Bradenton) is an excellent local resource. De Soto National Memorial. Palmetto Historical Park. Pre-1855 records in Hillsborough.
